task: The regex in get_path_param_names (utils.py) uses a non-greedy quantifier. Review suggested the simpler greedy form reads more clearly; apply that change.
correctness_argument: The non-greedy `{(.*?)}` stops at the first closing brace, so each `{…}` group in the path is captured as a separate parameter name. A route with two or more parameters yields the correct set of names instead of one malformed token spanning the whole path.
original_function:
def get_path_param_names(path: str) -> set[str]:
    return set(re.findall("{(.*?)}", path))
